ddls:
CREATE TABLE "grades" (
  "grade_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each grade */,
  "grade_name" TEXT /* Name of the grade (e.g., G, F, E, D, C) */,
  "min_score" REAL /* Minimum score required for the grade */,
  "max_score" REAL /* Maximum score required for the grade */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the grade */,
  "criteria" TEXT /* Detailed criteria for the grade */,
  "threshold_type" TEXT /* Type of threshold (e.g., score-based, behavior-based) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("grade_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Customer's date of birth */,
  "employment_status" TEXT /* Employment status of the customer */,
  "income_level" REAL /* Annual income of the customer */,
  "credit_history_length" INTEGER /* Length of credit history in years */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "loans" (
  "loan_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each loan */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer applying for the loan */,
  "loan_amount" REAL /* Amount of the loan */,
  "interest_rate" REAL /* Interest rate of the loan */,
  "loan_term" INTEGER /* Term of the loan (in months) */,
  "loan_status" TEXT /* Current status of the loan */,
  "payment_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of loan payments */,
  "collateral_type" TEXT /* Type of collateral if applicable */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("loan_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_loans_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "credit_scores" (
  "credit_score_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each credit score */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "score" REAL /* Credit score of the customer */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the credit score was calculated */,
  "score_source" TEXT /* Source of the credit score */,
  "score_version" TEXT /* Version of the scoring model used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("credit_score_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_credit_scores_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "grade_distributions" (
  "grade_distribution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each grade distribution */,
  "grade_id" INTEGER /* ID of the grade */,
  "n_obs" INTEGER /* Number of observations for the grade */,
  "prop_good" REAL /* Proportion of good credit scores for the grade */,
  "prop_n_obs" REAL /* Proportion of observations for the grade */,
  "n_good" INTEGER /* Number of good credit scores for the grade */,
  "n_bad" INTEGER /* Number of bad credit scores for the grade */,
  "prop_n_good" REAL /* Proportion of good credit scores for the grade (normalized) */,
  "prop_n_bad" REAL /* Proportion of bad credit scores for the grade (normalized) */,
  "WoE" REAL /* Weight of Evidence (WoE) for the grade */,
  "distribution_period" TEXT /* Time period for the distribution */,
  "model_version" TEXT /* Version of the model used for distribution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("grade_distribution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_grade_distributions_grade_id FOREIGN KEY ("grade_id") REFERENCES grades ("grade_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of model */,
  "training_data_size" INTEGER /* Size of the training dataset */,
  "performance_metrics" TEXT /* Key performance metrics */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_results" (
  "model_result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model result */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model used */,
  "score" REAL /* Credit score calculated by the model */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the score was calculated */,
  "result_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the model result */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_results_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_results_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "risk_levels" (
  "risk_level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each risk level */,
  "grade_id" INTEGER /* ID of the grade */,
  "risk_level" TEXT /* Risk level for the grade (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the risk level */,
  "risk_threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the risk level */,
  "risk_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the risk level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("risk_level_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_risk_levels_grade_id FOREIGN KEY ("grade_id") REFERENCES grades ("grade_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., risk analyst, underwriter, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the system was accessed */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time of access */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the system was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audits" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit event */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer affected by the change */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model affected by the change */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "change" TEXT /* Description of the change */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who made the change */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audits_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audits_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audits_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)
